mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate triggers a decrease in labor demand, which compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in post-shock periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the housing market triggers a decrease in demand for goods and services, which compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: > 900k units sold in the previous month
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in housing-market-downturn periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in industrial production triggers an expansion of supply, which compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: > 105k units produced in the previous month
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in late-cycle periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the gross domestic product triggers a decrease in demand for goods and services, which compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: > $16 trillion GDP in the previous quarter
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in recession periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the labor force participation rate triggers an expansion of supply, which compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: > 140k new jobs added in the previous month
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansionary-monetary-policy periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the consumer price index triggers a decrease in demand for goods and services, which compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: > 200 index points in the previous quarter
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in inflationary-pressures periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate triggers an easing monetary cycle, which compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate triggers a decrease in labor demand, which compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in post-recession periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
